events.description: Senator Murray introduced S. 716 to authorize issuance of patent in fee to George Peters for his allotted land on Crow Indian Reservation
S. 716 read twice and referred to the Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
Department of the Interior issued report on S. 716, recommending enactment if amended to allow sale rather than patent in fee
Chairman O'Mahoney sent letter to Senator Murray attaching Department of Interior report on S. 716 and requesting advice on amendments
Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs amended S. 716 at suggestion of Department of Interior to permit Secretary to sell land under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee
Committee on Public Lands reported S. 716 favorably without amendment and recommended bill do pass; committed to Committee of the Whole House and ordered printed as Report No. 508
Senator Murray wrote to George Peters advising that S. 716 passed the Senate on April 11th and had been reported favorably out of House Committee on Public Lands; enclosed copy of House report
S. 716 passed the House and cleared for President's signature
Telegram sent to George Peters in Wyola, Montana advising that S. 716 passed House and cleared for President's signature
Senator Murray wrote to George Peters in reply to his January 20th letter, advising he had prepared and was introducing a new bill to authorize issuance of patent in fee
George Peters indicated desire to sell his land to George Redfield, another Crow Indian

financial_transactions.description: Proposed sale of 840 acres of George Peters' allotted land on Crow Indian Reservation to a Crow Indian under existing regulations as authorized by amended S. 716
